name: Week 2 Push (Chest/Shoulders/Triceps)
description: Same core lifts as Week 1 — beat your numbers. Swaps: pec deck replaces cable fly (constant tension, easier to load), overhead tricep extension replaces pushdown (hits long head). Station-based grouping.

exercises.notes: Easy reps, get blood flowing to chest and tris
W1 you used DB bench at 50lbs. Try barbell if rack is free, otherwise DB bench — aim for 4x8 this week.
W1 working weight: 45lbs. Aim for 4x8 minimum, push toward 10 if you can.
W1 working weight: 40lbs. Aim for 3x8 clean reps before going up.
W1 working weight: 15lbs. Hit 3x15 before bumping to 17.5 or 20.
NEW — replaces cable fly. Constant tension through full ROM. Squeeze hard at center, slow stretch on the way back.
W1 working weight: 12.5lbs. Single arm cross-body. Constant tension.

NEW — replaces pushdown. HIGH cable, bench ~45° facing away (or kneel at cable column facing away). Hands near chest, extend out. Hits lats + tricep long head. Go lighter than you think. Video: x.com/deanttraining/status/2027798729642655818

tips: Same core lifts as Week 1 — your job is to beat your numbers
Two swaps: pec deck for cable fly (better tension curve), overhead tricep extension for pushdown (hits long head)
If pec deck is taken, cable fly is fine — just use the same weight from W1 and add reps
Overhead tricep extension: start light, the stretch is intense. 60-70% of your pushdown weight.
Rest 3 min between bench sets, 2 min between shoulder press, 75s between cable/isolation sets
If a machine is taken, check Training Notes for swap options
Total volume: ~16 working sets for push muscles
